channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Polymarket tries to woo a skeptical D.C. with a prediction market-themed bar
item.description: One of the largest prediction market platforms, Polymarket, opened a pop-up bar in the heart of Washington, D.C.'s lobbying district.
item.content:encoded: One of the largest prediction market platforms, Polymarket, opened a pop-up bar in the heart of Washington, D.C.'s lobbying district.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: How long will the war last? No one knows, and it's making oil prices weird
item.description: It's like the "Schrödinger's cat" thought experiment. There are two very different potential realities, and traders don't yet know which one is true.
item.content:encoded:
It's like the "Schrödinger's cat" thought experiment. There are two very different potential realities, and traders don't yet know which one is true.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump administration's Anthropic ban
item.description: The order briefly stops the government from labeling tech company Anthropic a "supply chain risk," calling that "classic First Amendment retaliation."
item.content:encoded:
The order briefly stops the government from labeling tech company Anthropic a "supply chain risk," calling that "classic First Amendment retaliation."

(Image credit: RICCARDO MILANI/Hans Lucas)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: The first major strike at a U.S. meatpacking plant in decades is in its second week
item.description: The first major strike at a U.S. meatpacking plant since 1985 is now in its second week in Greeley, Colo. The JBS beef plant there produces about 8% of the country's beef supply.
item.content:encoded: The first major strike at a U.S. meatpacking plant since 1985 is now in its second week in Greeley, Colo. The JBS beef plant there produces about 8% of the country's beef supply.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Mulholland

item.title: Higher oil prices are already affecting American businesses
item.description: Gasoline prices have jumped about a dollar a gallon since the war with Iran began. And diesel prices are up even more. That's tough on truckers, fishermen, and just about everyone else.
item.content:encoded: Gasoline prices have jumped about a dollar a gallon since the war with Iran began. And diesel prices are up even more. That's tough on truckers, fishermen, and just about everyone else.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: As TSA agents miss another paycheck, what's happening at airports with private security?
item.description: At 20 airports around the U.S., security screeners are getting paid as usual despite the ongoing DHS shutdown — because they're private contractors. Will more airports look at privatizing security?
item.content:encoded: At 20 airports around the U.S., security screeners are getting paid as usual despite the ongoing DHS shutdown — because they're private contractors. Will more airports look at privatizing security?
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Trump's attacks on offshore wind could hurt infrastructure spending across the economy
item.description: President Trump has tried to kill offshore wind's future in the U.S. But industry analysts say the attacks could hurt business confidence across the U.S. economy.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has tried to kill offshore wind's future in the U.S. But industry analysts say the attacks could hurt business confidence across the U.S. economy.

(Image credit: DON EMMERT/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: It's Equal Pay Day. Women have lost ground for the second year in a row
item.description: The annual observance marks how far into the new year women must work to make what men earned in the previous year. This year, it's March 26, a day later than it was in 2025.
item.content:encoded:
The annual observance marks how far into the new year women must work to make what men earned in the previous year. This year, it's March 26, a day later than it was in 2025.

(Image credit: simplehappyart)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: War with Iran disrupts fertilizer exports as U.S. farmers prepare for planting season
item.description: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.content:encoded:
Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.

(Image credit: Frank Morris)
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: OpenAI's Sora app may be going away, but its legacy will be the spread of AI video slop
item.description: Barely six months after its launch, OpenAI is ending an app that could generate AI video at the click of a button.
item.content:encoded: Barely six months after its launch, OpenAI is ending an app that could generate AI video at the click of a button.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel
